evidence.title: SDNY May 6, 2026 order granting unsealing of Epstein Note in United States v. Tartaglione
evidence.source_description: Text extraction from official Southern District of New York PDF order, United States v. Nicholas Tartaglione, Case No. 16-CR-832 (KMK), Order by Judge Kenneth M. Karas dated May 6, 2026.
evidence.relevance: Primary court source establishing that the New York Times sought to unseal a note described as a suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein; that the court granted unsealing of the Note; and that the Note was subject to public access.

evidence.title: People May 6, 2026 report: verification caveats and note provenance
evidence.source_description: Captured relevant text from People article by Charmaine Patterson, published May 6, 2026 9:01 PM EDT.
evidence.relevance: Contemporaneous public report before the May 8 deadline corroborating unsealing, note wording, and key adverse caveats that the document was not dated, signed, or verified and came through Tartaglione.

evidence.title: FOX Local May 6, 2026 report: Epstein purported suicide note unsealed and full text reported
evidence.source_description: Captured relevant text from FOX Local/FOX 13 Tampa Bay article by Catherine Stoddard, published May 6, 2026 7:30 PM EDT.
evidence.relevance: Contemporaneous public report before the May 8 deadline stating a federal judge unsealed the purported Epstein suicide note and reproducing the note's apparent text.

evidence.title: PBS/AP May 6-7, 2026 report: authorship uncertainty and DOJ-report omission
evidence.source_description: Relevant captured text from PBS NewsHour article by Associated Press reporters Michael R. Sisak and Larry Neumeister, published May 6, 2026 8:01 PM EDT and updated May 7, 2026 5:36 PM EDT.
evidence.relevance: Adverse/limiting contemporaneous reporting on the alleged note's provenance, public release, unclear authorship, absence from government death reports and DOJ file releases, and separate official death-cell note that officials said did not appear to be a suicide note.
